## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  First ACG audit pilot — detect-only judgment work;
  the guide is on trial as much as the documents.
  
  ## Gate
  Top-tier model only (Fable-class at slating), worked in the operator's chat —
  never mount or delegate this pace at a lesser tier.
  The pilot's primary deliverable is ACG amendment judgment —
  noticing where the guide is vague, wrong, or silent —
  and delegation makes that feedback secondhand.
  If mounted on a lesser tier, stop and tell the operator instead of proceeding.

  Audit the sentry pair —
  RBJS (rbjs_sentry.sh, shipped in the common-sentry-context vessel build
  context) against RBSSR + RBSSS —
  under ACG's detect-only moves
  (ACGm_104 conceptual-comment eviction, ACGm_106 ornament drain;
  note ACGm_105/107 findings in passing for the corpus sweeps).
  Twin deliverable: the findings report,
  and ACG amendments wherever the guide proved vague, wrong, or silent —
  pilots harden the guide.
  
  ## Cinched
  - Per-pair shape: source side and spec side read together;
    a relocation's receiving quoin is judged against this pair's spec.
  - Detect-only licensing per ACG: report findings;
    relocations land one reviewed move at a time, never batch.
  - This pair's dense security-rationale comments are the deliberate test of the
    edit-time/Palisade boundary —
    whether why-comments at the iptables/conntrack sites are evictable
    design-time knowledge or legitimate Palisade characterization.
    Settle with the operator; record the ruling as an ACG amendment.
  
  ## Done when
  Findings report exists with each finding tagged by move-type and receiving home;
  agreed relocations/drains landed as individually reviewed moves;
  ACG amendments landed;
  fast suite green if any source file changed.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣Bb
2	
3	## Shape
4	
5	Pre-MVP source/spec hygiene grown into the ACG audit program, in two strata.
6	First, targeted allocation sweeps feathered in pace-at-a-time alongside MVP
7	feature finalization — magic-string -> constant sweeps and noun-cluster
8	curation, each with a blast radius that merges trivially.
9	Second, the audit program: per-pair spec/module audits under the ACG move
10	catalog, run while ACG is young so the guide and the documents harden together.
11	A seed, not a tree: scope stays tight to the source/spec allocation family.
12	Resist becoming the junk drawer for unrelated cleanups.
13	
14	## Governing idea
15	
16	The authority is ACG (Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/ACG-AllocationCodingGuide.md) —
17	the spine, the three homes, the move discipline, and the numbered move catalog
18	all live there.
19	This paddock cites and never restates
20	(ACG's statements -> definition-sites clause applied to itself;
21	an earlier revision of this paddock paraphrased the guide and has been drained).
22	
23	## Cinched
24	
25	- ACG = "Allocation Coding Guide" (not "Asciidoc" — the guide governs source
26	  authoring/allocation; asciidoc is MCM's domain).
27	  Veiled/proprietary, guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG.
28	  Not released at MVP.
29	- v1 posture: bless and name emergent practice; no mandated universals;
30	  no qualify-time enforcement.
31	  Universals are candidates confirmed pace-by-pace.
32	- Audit pace shape: one pace = one spec-subdocument/bash-module pair,
33	  source-side and spec-side moves applied in one read;
34	  the inherently cross-document moves (paraphrase collapse, tidemark drain)
35	  get single corpus-wide sweep paces instead.
36	- Pilot posture: ACG is untested — three pilots run before any roster of pair
37	  paces is slated.
38	  Every pilot's deliverable is twofold: the findings,
39	  and the ACG amendments where the guide proved vague, wrong, or silent.
40	  Roster vs further pilots is a post-pilot groom decision.
41	- Pilot selection (groom survey): the sentry pair tests the edit-time/Palisade
42	  comment boundary;
43	  the access-probe pair carries known-positive paraphrase/ornament specimens,
44	  so the audit proves itself against a known answer;
45	  the conjure pair is the richest surface and the scale test,
46	  gated with the rest of the ₣BH lane.
47	- Pilots execute at top model tier in the operator's chat;
48	  delegation to lesser tiers is for the post-pilot roster only,
49	  after ACG hardens.
50	- Per-pace model tier rides in each docket's Character line
51	  (judgment/curation work at opus, the mechanical sweeps at sonnet);
52	  the pilot top-tier gate stays a hard Gate, not a Character hint.
53	- Antipattern catalog: mcm_antipattern broadens to cover delivery defects as
54	  well as document defects;
55	  Stun, Molehill, and word cancer all home in MCM's Antipatterns section,
56	  with ACG and the JJK gradient-delivery guidance citing rather than defining.
57	
58	## Sequencing & lanes
59	
60	Ungated front, in order: the antipattern-catalog pace, then the sentry pilot,
61	then the access-probe pilot —
62	the antipattern pace goes first because the access-probe pilot's word-cancer
63	findings cite the home it establishes.
64	The BUK test-vocab mint and the axvo symbol-lookahead amendment ride the back
65	of the ungated front:
66	the mint is self-contained in BUS0,
67	and the axvo pace is only partially gated —
68	its RBS0 migration share defers until ₣BH lands.
69	
70	The rest of the heat is gated on ₣BH:
71	
72	- Eviction (removing superseded terms) — ₣BH curates the KILL/KEEP list;
73	  the standing conformance engine's rows populate per-cluster behind ₣BH's
74	  cutovers.
75	  ACG names that engine the canonical home for evicted-term assertions.
76	- The conjure pilot — its surface is under active ₣BH rewrite;
77	  findings against it now would go stale.
78	- The rbrv.env single-home sweeps and the noun-cluster curation —
79	  their files share ₣BH's rewrite surface.
80	- Cluster repair (curating living vocabulary to a single home) is disjoint from
81	  eviction and never removes terms.
82	
83	Triage rider across all audit work:
84	Manor / Depot / account-role vocabulary is ₣BZ's lane and
85	reliquary / Lode / Hallmark vocabulary is ₣BH's — report, never touch.
86	Verb clusters stay unslated until their owning heats land;
87	slating against terms two hot heats are still moving would re-create the drift
88	this heat exists to kill.
89	
90	## Done looks like
91	
92	ACG hardened through three audit pilots, amendments landed;
93	the antipattern catalog homed in MCM with citers repointed;
94	the rbrv.env literal single-homed in bash and rust as the first worked
95	ACGm_101 application;
96	the noun clusters curated to single homes;
97	the post-pilot groom has decided roster vs further pilots.
98

100	
101	## Character
102	First ACG audit pilot — detect-only judgment work;
103	the guide is on trial as much as the documents.
104

112

116	context) against RBSSR + RBSSS —
117	under ACG's detect-only moves
118	(ACGm_104 conceptual-comment eviction, ACGm_106 ornament drain;
119	note ACGm_105/107 findings in passing for the corpus sweeps).
120	Twin deliverable: the findings report,
121	and ACG amendments wherever the guide proved vague, wrong, or silent —
122	pilots harden the guide.

127	- Detect-only licensing per ACG: report findings;

# jjezs_paddock ₣Bb

## Shape

Pre-MVP source/spec hygiene grown into the ACG audit program, in two strata.
First, targeted allocation sweeps feathered in pace-at-a-time alongside MVP
feature finalization — magic-string -> constant sweeps and noun-cluster
curation, each with a blast radius that merges trivially.
Second, the audit program: per-pair spec/module audits under the ACG move
catalog, run while ACG is young so the guide and the documents harden together.
A seed, not a tree: scope stays tight to the source/spec allocation family.
Resist becoming the junk drawer for unrelated cleanups.

## Governing idea

The authority is ACG (Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/ACG-AllocationCodingGuide.md) —
the spine, the three homes, the move discipline, and the numbered move catalog
all live there.
This paddock cites and never restates
(ACG's statements -> definition-sites clause applied to itself;
an earlier revision of this paddock paraphrased the guide and has been drained).

## Cinched

- ACG = "Allocation Coding Guide" (not "Asciidoc" — the guide governs source
  authoring/allocation; asciidoc is MCM's domain).
  Veiled/proprietary, guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG.
  Not released at MVP.
- v1 posture: bless and name emergent practice; no mandated universals;
  no qualify-time enforcement.
  Universals are candidates confirmed pace-by-pace.
- Audit pace shape: one pace = one spec-subdocument/bash-module pair,
  source-side and spec-side moves applied in one read;
  the inherently cross-document moves (paraphrase collapse, tidemark drain)
  get single corpus-wide sweep paces instead.
- Pilot posture: ACG is untested — three pilots run before any roster of pair
  paces is slated.
  Every pilot's deliverable is twofold: the findings,
  and the ACG amendments where the guide proved vague, wrong, or silent.
  Roster vs further pilots is a post-pilot groom decision.
- Pilot selection (groom survey): the sentry pair tests the edit-time/Palisade
  comment boundary;
  the access-probe pair carries known-positive paraphrase/ornament specimens,
  so the audit proves itself against a known answer;
  the conjure pair is the richest surface and the scale test,
  gated with the rest of the ₣BH lane.
- Pilots execute at top model tier in the operator's chat;
  delegation to lesser tiers is for the post-pilot roster only,
  after ACG hardens.
- Per-pace model tier rides in each docket's Character line
  (judgment/curation work at opus, the mechanical sweeps at sonnet);
  the pilot top-tier gate stays a hard Gate, not a Character hint.
- Antipattern catalog: mcm_antipattern broadens to cover delivery defects as
  well as document defects;
  Stun, Molehill, and word cancer all home in MCM's Antipatterns section,
  with ACG and the JJK gradient-delivery guidance citing rather than defining.

## Sequencing & lanes

Ungated front, in order: the antipattern-catalog pace, then the sentry pilot,
then the access-probe pilot —
the antipattern pace goes first because the access-probe pilot's word-cancer
findings cite the home it establishes.
The BUK test-vocab mint and the axvo symbol-lookahead amendment ride the back
of the ungated front:
the mint is self-contained in BUS0,
and the axvo pace is only partially gated —
its RBS0 migration share defers until ₣BH lands.

The rest of the heat is gated on ₣BH:

- Eviction (removing superseded terms) — ₣BH curates the KILL/KEEP list;
  the standing conformance engine's rows populate per-cluster behind ₣BH's
  cutovers.
  ACG names that engine the canonical home for evicted-term assertions.
- The conjure pilot — its surface is under active ₣BH rewrite;
  findings against it now would go stale.
- The rbrv.env single-home sweeps and the noun-cluster curation —
  their files share ₣BH's rewrite surface.
- Cluster repair (curating living vocabulary to a single home) is disjoint from
  eviction and never removes terms.

Triage rider across all audit work:
Manor / Depot / account-role vocabulary is ₣BZ's lane and
reliquary / Lode / Hallmark vocabulary is ₣BH's — report, never touch.
Verb clusters stay unslated until their owning heats land;
slating against terms two hot heats are still moving would re-create the drift
this heat exists to kill.

## Done looks like

ACG hardened through three audit pilots, amendments landed;
the antipattern catalog homed in MCM with citers repointed;
the rbrv.env literal single-homed in bash and rust as the first worked
ACGm_101 application;
the noun clusters curated to single homes;
the post-pilot groom has decided roster vs further pilots.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAI

## Character
First ACG audit pilot — detect-only judgment work;
the guide is on trial as much as the documents.

## Gate
Top-tier model only (Fable-class at slating), worked in the operator's chat —
never mount or delegate this pace at a lesser tier.
The pilot's primary deliverable is ACG amendment judgment —
noticing where the guide is vague, wrong, or silent —
and delegation makes that feedback secondhand.
If mounted on a lesser tier, stop and tell the operator instead of proceeding.

## Goal
Audit the sentry pair —
RBJS (rbjs_sentry.sh, shipped in the common-sentry-context vessel build
context) against RBSSR + RBSSS —
under ACG's detect-only moves
(ACGm_104 conceptual-comment eviction, ACGm_106 ornament drain;
note ACGm_105/107 findings in passing for the corpus sweeps).
Twin deliverable: the findings report,
and ACG amendments wherever the guide proved vague, wrong, or silent —
pilots harden the guide.

## Cinched
- Per-pair shape: source side and spec side read together;
  a relocation's receiving quoin is judged against this pair's spec.
- Detect-only licensing per ACG: report findings;
  relocations land one reviewed move at a time, never batch.
- This pair's dense security-rationale comments are the deliberate test of the
  edit-time/Palisade boundary —
  whether why-comments at the iptables/conntrack sites are evictable
  design-time knowledge or legitimate Palisade characterization.
  Settle with the operator; record the ruling as an ACG amendment.

## Done when
Findings report exists with each finding tagged by move-type and receiving home;
agreed relocations/drains landed as individually reviewed moves;
ACG amendments landed;
fast suite green if any source file changed.

content.content:
1	<!--
2	Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
3	All rights reserved.
4	SPDX-License-Identifier: LicenseRef-Proprietary
5	-->
6	
7	# Allocation Coding Guide (ACG) — Reference the Home
8	
9	## Purpose
10	
11	ACG codifies one discipline: **don't recreate inline what already has a named
12	home — reference the home.** It is the language-neutral, allocation-facing
13	sibling to BCG (host bash), RCG (host Rust), WSG (Windows transport), and CBG
14	(cloud step). Those govern *how* code in one environment is written; ACG governs
15	*where a fact or a name is allowed to live*, and what source must do when a home
16	already exists: point at it, never restate it.
17	
18	ACG is the **source-side complement to MCM**. MCM builds the named homes — quoins
19	in specs, constants in code; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather
20	than recreate them. That complement is why ACG lives in the CMK kit beside MCM
21	and AXLA rather than in any single-language kit.
22	
23	ACG is **v1**: it blesses and names emergent practice and states the spine. It
24	does **not** mandate universals ("every verb must announce"); those are
25	candidates, confirmed move-by-move, not law. There is no qualify-time enforcement
26	in v1. Every move-type below is licensed precisely — most are detect-and-report,
27	not repair.
28	
29	## How this document is organized — frame, then catalog
30	
31	ACG separates two kinds of knowledge, because they earn their form differently:
32	
33	- **The Frame** (prose, no IDs) — the spine, the three homes, the v1 posture, the
34	  move discipline. Systematic and interderivable; internalized, not cited by
35	  number.
36	- **The Move Catalog** (numbered `ACGm_`) — discrete, verifier-backed move-types,
37	  each of which something *points at*: a constant's breadcrumb comment, a
38	  detect-and-report verb, a conformance-engine row, a review flagging a
39	  violation. An ID earns its existence only when a citer will exist.
40	
41	This split is itself the load-bearing test (BCG Core Philosophy): catalog the
42	discrete moves; systematize the discipline behind them.
43	
44	## The spine — reference the home
45	
46	The governing idea, stated once:
47	
48	> Don't recreate inline what has a named home. **Values → constants; concepts →
49	> quoin-refs; statements → definition sites.** MCM builds the homes; ACG governs
50	> source's duty to reference rather than recreate.
51	
52	This is the **allocation instance of Load-Bearing Complexity** (CLAUDE.md). A
53	recreated value or name is a non-load-bearing duplicate: its existence adds drift
54	risk without adding correctness, and the drift it invites is exactly the gap
55	between intent and behavior the principle forbids. BCG's and RCG's Constant
56	Discipline and Interface Contamination are the per-language instances of this;
57	ACG is the general statement those instantiate, plus the one thing a
58	single-language guide cannot state — *where prose with no code-home actually
59	goes.*
60	
61	## The three homes — allocate by when-read
62	
63	A fact has exactly one right home, fixed by *when it is read*:
64	
65	| When read | Home | Carries |
66	|-----------|------|---------|
67	| **Design-time** | the spec (MCM quoin) | what a thing *is* and why it is shaped so — the conceptual model |
68	| **Edit-time** | the source comment | operational mechanics only — language idiom, and the Palisade (foreign-boundary) characterization a maintainer needs *at the code* |
69	| **Execution-time** | the runtime announcement | what is happening now — intent printed as the operation runs |
70	| **History-time** | the commit | when and why a thing changed — git log and blame own it; version narration in the document body is a skidmark (MCM `mcm_skidmark`) |
71	
72	Most conceptual comments are **temporal misallocation**: design-time or
73	execution-time knowledge dumped into the edit-time medium, where it rots for lack
74	of a forcing function. The spec is re-read when the design is questioned; the
75	announcement is re-read every run; an edit-time comment restating either is read
76	by no one with a reason to correct it when it drifts.
77	
78	Two edit-time/execution-time source-doc forms are distinct and **both blessed**:
79	
80	- **Contract header** — a bounded edit-time comment stating a function's input
81	  contract and invariants. Read when editing the function.
82	- **Intent announcement** — an execution-time printout naming what the operation
83	  is doing. Read when running it.
84	
85	Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-parameter-shows-the-options
86	practice, live in `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil`) is blessed alongside happy-path
87	announcement: an error that lists the valid forms is an execution-time
88	announcement of the contract, not contamination.
89	
90	**The execution-time home is plural off the terminal.** "Announcement" reads as
91	a single printed stream because a CLI gives you one. A routine whose product
92	does not go to a terminal — an MCP command, a library call, a daemon — splits
93	it: the **product** (the value returned to the caller), the **trace**
94	(diagnostic breadcrumbs), and the **error** travel different media, bound per
95	project. Naming the full output-role roster is future work; one invariant earns
96	its place now: **trace has no re-read-every-run forcing function, so it rots like
97	an edit-time comment — never make it the sole home of design-time knowledge.**
98	
99	## The third clause — statements → definition sites
100	
101	The spine governs prose, not only values and names. Every normative
102	idea — a rule, a scope claim, a constraint, a rationale — has exactly one
103	authoritative wording at one definition site. Every other appearance is a
104	**citation**: it may name the home and orient the reader; it may not restate
105	the content in other words.
106	
107	A paraphrase is a fork — a second wording that drifts independently, leaving a
108	reader unable to know which wording is law. Its metastatic form is the
109	**word cancer** (concept home: MCM `mcm_word_cancer`) — the most recurrent
110	failure mode of LLM-assisted maintenance of large codebases, and the defect
111	this clause exists to forbid. A quoin gives a concept one string; this clause
112	gives a claim one wording — the prose analogue of the prefix-collision
113	mechanism in `Memos/retired/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`.
114	Canonical specimen: the 2026-06-10 Diff-Friendly-Prose repair, where a context
115	file *characterized* a spec's scope instead of citing it, and the
116	characterization carried the error for months.
117	
118	**Normative register** rides this clause on the spec side. In a spec, every
119	sentence either constrains — an implementer or validator could act differently
120	because it exists — or is explicitly marked as rationale or gestalt (MCM's task
121	lens `**Gestalt**:` field is the blessed form: metaphor marked as interpretive
122	guidance). Unmarked ornament is contamination: it spends reader attention and
123	implies constraint where none exists. A code comment restating design-time
124	knowledge is the same failure in the other medium (temporal misallocation,
125	above); flowery spec prose is its mirror.
126	
127	## The fourth clause — wire formats are named homes
128	
129	A JSON document this project authors on **both sides** — an envelope, a result
130	slot, a substitutions blob — is itself a home: a wire format with senders and
131	receivers that must stay findable from each other. Its keys are therefore
132	**minted names** (CLAUDE.md mint: any persistent name anywhere is in scope),
133	carrying one key-sprue per format: `rblv_digest`, never bare `digest`. The sprue
134	is what makes the format greppable — `grep rblv_` returns the author, every
135	consumer, and the schema definition, with no central registry to drift (the
136	`buo` tweak-sprue precedent; the `_RBGL_` substitution registers are the senior
137	worked example). One sprue per *format*, not per file or per domain: two formats
138	sharing a sprue blur the census that is the sprue's whole purpose.
139	
140	The boundary is the Palisade: JSON conforming to a **foreign** schema — a Cloud
141	Build envelope, an OCI manifest, a vendor API response — keeps the foreign keys.
142	Sprued keys inside a foreign document are the tell that someone is decorating a
143	schema they don't own.
144	
145	The clause is prospective: it binds every wire format as it is authored or
146	touched; no retroactive sweep is mandated. The conversion move is catalogued as
147	ACGm_108.
148	
149	## The move discipline — one litmus, three rules
150	
151	Every cleanup move obeys one litmus:
152	
153	> **Mutate only where a *wrong* move is cheaply caught; everywhere else the verb
154	> is detect-and-report, never repair.**
155	
156	Code-side moves — the value resolves through a named constant, and shellcheck
157	plus the fast suite verify it — may mutate now. Spec- and document-side moves are
158	detect-and-report until the lexer/linter that would catch a wrong move exists.
159	
160	Three rules ride that litmus:
161	
162	1. **Triage is part of every move, not an afterthought.** Exclude the
163	   false-positive class first; declare the authoritative side before any
164	   alignment; gate scope to settled lanes — never terms a hot heat is still
165	   moving. A sweep run against vocabulary two heats are still relocating only
166	   re-creates the drift it meant to kill.
167	2. **A checker proves itself against a known answer before its output is
168	   trusted.** The conformance fixture (next section) is the canonical home for
169	   such checks — ACG eating its own dog food: a named home, referenced not
170	   recreated.
171	3. **Concept linkage is name-identity.** The implementing symbol *is* the link: a
172	   function name equals its quoin's display-text, a tabtarget filename is the
173	   quoin's sprue, a scoped method is its inlay. A source-to-spec comment is the
174	   *exception*, reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.
175	
176	## The named home — the conformance fixture
177	
178	Rule 2 and move-types ACGm_102/103 all defer to one concrete home: the
179	**`conformance`** fast-tier theurge fixture
180	(`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrn_conformance.rs`), registered in the `fast` suite. It
181	is to evicted vocabulary what BUK's `bug_require_clean_tree` is to the clean-tree
182	gate — the single named place the check lives, so no caller improvises its own.
183	
184	The fixture is a data-driven engine, not a word list. It reads rows of
185	`{kill_stem, keep_contexts}` and scans `Tools/` and `tt/` with identifier-boundary
186	awareness, flagging a stem as a bare token while sparing it inside a kept
187	identifier (`Identifier`) or under an exempt path (`PathPrefix`). A row with empty
188	`keep_contexts` is a *pure corpse* — the term must appear nowhere. The engine
189	proves itself against known inputs before its verdict on the live tree is trusted
190	(move discipline, rule 2), so it ships proven with zero production rows.
191	
192	**The rule: retire a term → add a row there. Never improvise a grep.** Population
193	is deliberately deferred — each term gains its row behind its own cutover, owned
194	by the heat retiring it — never against vocabulary a hot heat is still moving
195	(move discipline, rule 1).
196	
197	## The Move Catalog
198	
199	Each move-type states five things: **Detect** (the rule that finds a site),
200	**Authority** (the ground truth a site is judged against), **Licensing**,
201	**Verifier**, and a countable **Done**. Headers tag the licensing: **🔧
202	mutate-now** (a wrong move is cheaply caught — repair in place) or **🔍
203	detect-only** (read and report; no repair until a verifier exists). Numbered from
204	101 to leave room for insertion; once a move has a citer it is never renumbered.
205	
206	### 🔧 ACGm_101: magic-string → constant
207	
208	- **Detect:** a literal value — a path fragment, a filename, a repeated number or
209	  string — that *constructs or addresses* something, where a named home exists or
210	  should. Not human-facing prose.
211	- **Authority:** the value resolves through a single named constant home (e.g.
212	  the `RBCC_rbr*_file` family in `rbcc_constants.sh`). The constant is ground
213	  truth; every literal is a copy that can drift.
214	- **Licensing:** mutate-now — the constant either resolves or the verifier fails,
215	  so a wrong move dies loud and immediately.
216	- **Verifier:** bash — `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh` + `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh`
217	  green; Rust — `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
218	- **Done:** every genuine construction site resolves through the constant; the
219	  constant carries a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
220	- **Triage:** naming a file *for a human* in a message is not a magic string —
221	  `doc_params`, `buc_info`/`buh_line` messages, and comments keep the literal.
222	  Exclude that class before sweeping.
223	- **Worked example:** the `rbrv.env` filename sweep is the first worked
224	  application of this move.
225	
226	### 🔍 ACGm_102: name-identity
227	
228	- **Detect:** an operation quoin (voiced `//axvo_procedure` or `//axvo_method`)
229	  whose display-text and implementing symbol disagree.
230	- **Authority:** the implementing symbol is the link (move discipline, rule 3) —
231	  a procedure's function name equals its quoin's display-text; a source-to-spec
232	  comment is the exception where no single symbol can carry it.
233	- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side; no mutation until the lexer/linter
234	  exists.
235	- **Verifier:** none mechanized in v1 — read and reported by hand. The
236	  conformance fixture is the future verifier.
237	- **Done:** a report of operation-quoin / symbol disagreements; nothing mutated.
238	- **Formal dependency:** full mechanical decidability waits on an AXLA arity
239	  change — an explicit symbol lookahead on `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method`
240	  (one `mcm_inlay`, the prefix-disciplined source symbol; mirroring
241	  `axvo_tabtarget`'s literal lookahead). That change carries a migration across
242	  every live operation voicing, so it is deferred to its own pace (see *The
243	  AXLA/MCM interface*). Until it lands, the move is read against the existing
244	  `axvo_*` voicings — which already mark *which* quoins are operations — plus the
245	  implementation symbol named at an operation's detail site (`axhems_scoped_method`).
246	
247	### 🔍 ACGm_103: typed-parameter lookahead
248	
249	- **Detect:** a parameter whose type is resolvable by bounded lookahead but is
250	  not yet carried as a type quoin-ref.
251	- **Authority:** the lookahead scanning rule (below) — the parameter's type is
252	  the attribute-reference the marker reads.
253	- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side.
254	- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter.
255	- **Done:** a report of parameters whose type is derivable but unhomed; nothing
256	  mutated.
257	
258	### 🔍 ACGm_104: conceptual-comment eviction
259	
260	- **Detect:** an edit-time comment carrying design-time knowledge — what a thing
261	  *is*, why it is shaped so, how it relates to other concepts — rather than
262	  operational mechanics or Palisade characterization.
263	- **Authority:** the three-homes table. The spec quoin is the design-time home;
264	  where no quoin exists, the move creates or extends one, then cites it.
265	- **Licensing:** detect-only for the sweep — relocation mutates two documents
266	  (source and spec) and a wrong move is not cheaply caught, so each relocation
267	  lands as its own reviewed move, never batch-applied.
268	- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter plus spec link validation.
269	- **Done:** a report of misallocated comments, each tagged with its receiving
270	  quoin (existing or proposed); relocations land one reviewed move at a time.
271	
272	### 🔍 ACGm_105: paraphrase collapse
273	
274	- **Detect:** the same normative idea worded differently in two or more places —
275	  across specs, context files, handbooks, or comments. The smell: a reader could
276	  ask "which of these is law?"
277	- **Authority:** the statements → definition sites clause; the concept home is
278	  MCM's `mcm_word_cancer` quoin. Triage declares one home authoritative; every
279	  other site becomes a citation.
280	- **Licensing:** detect-only — the collapse mutates prose in several documents
281	  at once and judgment selects the surviving wording; never batch-applied.
282	- **Verifier:** none mechanized; recurrence is caught by re-running the detect
283	  sweep against the declared home.
284	- **Done:** for each word cancer: one authoritative wording at one site,
285	  citations elsewhere, the variant wordings gone.
286	
287	### 🔍 ACGm_106: ornament drain
288	
289	- **Detect:** spec sentences in normative position that neither constrain nor
290	  carry marked rationale or gestalt — prose an implementer or validator could
291	  delete without acting differently.
292	- **Authority:** the normative-register rule (the statements clause, above).
293	- **Licensing:** detect-only — whether a sentence constrains is judgment;
294	  draining is per-document and reviewed.
295	- **Verifier:** none in v1.
296	- **Done:** the spec reads as constraint plus marked rationale; deleted ornament
297	  is gone, demoted ornament is marked.
298	
299	### 🔍 ACGm_107: skidmark drain
300	
301	- **Detect:** version commentary in a durable document — revision markers, dated
302	  change narration, "new in vX" annotations. Durable means spec, guide, code, or
303	  context file; a dated artifact (memo, scar, commit message) is exempt — history
304	  is its content.
305	- **Authority:** the history-time row of the three-homes table; the concept home
306	  is MCM's `mcm_skidmark` quoin. The commit is the home — git log and blame carry
307	  the change story with a forcing function the document body lacks.
308	- **Licensing:** detect-only as a sweep; an individual drain is mutate-now when
309	  the deleted text is pure narration (the content survives in git), reviewed when
310	  narration is entangled with normative statements.
311	- **Verifier:** none in v1; candidate for a cheap regex pass in the future linter
312	  (date-shaped tokens and version markers outside memo paths).
313	- **Done:** durable documents read in the present tense; history questions answer
314	  through git.
315	
316	### 🔧 ACGm_108: bare wire-key → minted sprue
317	
318	- **Detect:** an RB-authored JSON wire format (both sender and receiver ours)
319	  whose keys are bare terms (`name`, `digest`, `stamp`) rather than sprue-carried
320	  minted names.
321	- **Authority:** the fourth clause (wire formats are named homes). The sprue
322	  itself is governed by the mint discipline (CLAUDE.md): one sprue per format,
323	  terminal-exclusive, used for nothing else.
324	- **Licensing:** mutate-now when the format's author and every consumer convert
325	  in one move and a live verifier covers the path — a rename either resolves
326	  everywhere or the suite fails loud. Detect-only when the consumers cannot all
327	  convert together; for an RB-authored format that inability is itself a finding
328	  worth recording.
329	- **Verifier:** the consuming suite tier for the format (e.g. the capture
330	  fixtures for the Lode vouch envelope), plus the census check: `grep <sprue>_`
331	  returns exactly the format's sites and nothing else.
332	- **Done:** every key in the format carries the sprue; the format's schema
333	  identifier (if it has one) is bumped; the sprue grep is the format's complete
334	  sender/receiver census. First worked application: the Lode vouch envelope
335	  (`rblv_`, schema `rbld-vouch-2`). Second worked application: the Lode slot/result
336	  format (`rbls_`, keys `rbls_slot_1`/`rbls_stamp`/`rbls_vouch`).
337	- **Triage:** foreign-schema documents are excluded by the Palisade boundary
338	  before any sweep. Pre-MVP formats convert with no dual-dialect reading — there
339	  is no compatibility surface; standing artifacts are re-captured or discarded,
340	  never grandfathered.
341	
342	## The AXLA/MCM interface
343	
344	### The lookahead scanning rule
345	
346	When counting a marker's lookahead in an MCM document, scan the following text and
347	**skip prose lines and blank lines; count only attribute-references (`{…}` linked
348	terms) and backtick sprues.** A dimension that counts "the Nth linked term" (e.g.
349	`axd_grouped`) counts attribute-references only — a backtick inlay or sprue is a
350	distinct token type and does not shift that count.
351	
352	### Name-identity's symbol-link dependency (deferred)
353	
354	ACGm_102 becomes mechanically decidable only when an operation voicing carries its
355	implementing symbol explicitly. The surveyed shape: add a one-`mcm_inlay`
356	lookahead to `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method` reading the literal source symbol
357	(the MCM-correct token for a prefix-disciplined name, matching how
358	`axhems_scoped_method` already reads an implementation method name). It is an
359	*amendment* to two established voicings with many live consumers, not a fresh
360	mint, so it carries a real migration and is held for its own deliberate pace
361	rather than folded into guide authoring. Recorded here as ACGm_102's named
362	dependency; raised and deferred, not dropped.
363	
364	## Related Guides
365	
366	- **BCG** — host bash. Constant Discipline and Interface Contamination are the
367	  bash instances of the spine.
368	- **RCG** — host Rust. Constant Discipline (Beyond Strings), String Boundary
369	  Discipline, and Interface Contamination are the Rust instances.
370	- **WSG**, **CBG** — foreign-environment siblings (Windows transport, cloud step).
371	- **MCM** — builds the named homes ACG references; the design-time home.
372	- **AXLA** — the motif vocabulary; the lookahead scanning rule and the
373	  name-identity symbol-link dependency live against it.
374	- **CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline" (mint)** — governs the naming homes
375	  themselves.
376	
377	## Acronym Registry
378	
379	| Term | Expansion |
380	|------|-----------|
381	| ACG | Allocation Coding Guide (this document) |
382	| The spine | "Reference the home" — values → constants, concepts → quoin-refs, statements → definition sites |
383	| Wire format | An RB-authored JSON document with senders and receivers; a named home whose keys are minted (sprued) names — the fourth clause |
384	| Word cancer | Variations of one normative idea scattered as paraphrases, each drifting independently; the metastatic violation of statements → definition sites. Quoin home: MCM `mcm_word_cancer` |
385	| Normative register | Spec-side rule: every sentence constrains or is marked rationale/gestalt; unmarked ornament is contamination |
386	| Skidmark | Version commentary stranded in a durable document; history-time knowledge whose home is the commit. Quoin home: MCM `mcm_skidmark` |
387	| Home | The one right location for a fact, fixed by when it is read (design / edit / execution time) |
388	| Move-type | A catalogued `ACGm_` cleanup move: detect-rule, authority, licensing, verifier, countable Done |
389	| mutate-now | Licensing: a wrong move is cheaply caught, so the verb may repair in place |
390	| detect-only | Licensing: the verb reads and reports; no repair until a verifier exists |
391	| Name-identity | Concept linkage where the implementing symbol *is* the link to its quoin |

<!--

# Allocation Coding Guide (ACG) — Reference the Home

## Purpose

ACG codifies one discipline: **don't recreate inline what already has a named
home — reference the home.** It is the language-neutral, allocation-facing
sibling to BCG (host bash), RCG (host Rust), WSG (Windows transport), and CBG
(cloud step). Those govern *how* code in one environment is written; ACG governs
*where a fact or a name is allowed to live*, and what source must do when a home
already exists: point at it, never restate it.

ACG is the **source-side complement to MCM**. MCM builds the named homes — quoins
in specs, constants in code; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather
than recreate them. That complement is why ACG lives in the CMK kit beside MCM
and AXLA rather than in any single-language kit.

ACG is **v1**: it blesses and names emergent practice and states the spine. It
does **not** mandate universals ("every verb must announce"); those are
candidates, confirmed move-by-move, not law. There is no qualify-time enforcement
in v1. Every move-type below is licensed precisely — most are detect-and-report,
not repair.

## How this document is organized — frame, then catalog

ACG separates two kinds of knowledge, because they earn their form differently:

- **The Frame** (prose, no IDs) — the spine, the three homes, the v1 posture, the
  move discipline. Systematic and interderivable; internalized, not cited by
  number.
- **The Move Catalog** (numbered `ACGm_`) — discrete, verifier-backed move-types,
  each of which something *points at*: a constant's breadcrumb comment, a
  detect-and-report verb, a conformance-engine row, a review flagging a
  violation. An ID earns its existence only when a citer will exist.

This split is itself the load-bearing test (BCG Core Philosophy): catalog the
discrete moves; systematize the discipline behind them.

## The spine — reference the home

The governing idea, stated once:

> Don't recreate inline what has a named home. **Values → constants; concepts →
> quoin-refs; statements → definition sites.** MCM builds the homes; ACG governs
> source's duty to reference rather than recreate.

This is the **allocation instance of Load-Bearing Complexity** (CLAUDE.md). A
recreated value or name is a non-load-bearing duplicate: its existence adds drift
risk without adding correctness, and the drift it invites is exactly the gap
between intent and behavior the principle forbids. BCG's and RCG's Constant
Discipline and Interface Contamination are the per-language instances of this;
ACG is the general statement those instantiate, plus the one thing a
single-language guide cannot state — *where prose with no code-home actually
goes.*

## The three homes — allocate by when-read

A fact has exactly one right home, fixed by *when it is read*:

| When read | Home | Carries |
|-----------|------|---------|
| **Design-time** | the spec (MCM quoin) | what a thing *is* and why it is shaped so — the conceptual model |
| **Edit-time** | the source comment | operational mechanics only — language idiom, and the Palisade (foreign-boundary) characterization a maintainer needs *at the code* |
| **Execution-time** | the runtime announcement | what is happening now — intent printed as the operation runs |
| **History-time** | the commit | when and why a thing changed — git log and blame own it; version narration in the document body is a skidmark (MCM `mcm_skidmark`) |

Most conceptual comments are **temporal misallocation**: design-time or
execution-time knowledge dumped into the edit-time medium, where it rots for lack
of a forcing function. The spec is re-read when the design is questioned; the
announcement is re-read every run; an edit-time comment restating either is read
by no one with a reason to correct it when it drifts.

Two edit-time/execution-time source-doc forms are distinct and **both blessed**:

- **Contract header** — a bounded edit-time comment stating a function's input
  contract and invariants. Read when editing the function.
- **Intent announcement** — an execution-time printout naming what the operation
  is doing. Read when running it.

Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-parameter-shows-the-options
practice, live in `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil`) is blessed alongside happy-path
announcement: an error that lists the valid forms is an execution-time
announcement of the contract, not contamination.

**The execution-time home is plural off the terminal.** "Announcement" reads as
a single printed stream because a CLI gives you one. A routine whose product
does not go to a terminal — an MCP command, a library call, a daemon — splits
it: the **product** (the value returned to the caller), the **trace**
(diagnostic breadcrumbs), and the **error** travel different media, bound per
project. Naming the full output-role roster is future work; one invariant earns
its place now: **trace has no re-read-every-run forcing function, so it rots like
an edit-time comment — never make it the sole home of design-time knowledge.**

## The third clause — statements → definition sites

The spine governs prose, not only values and names. Every normative
idea — a rule, a scope claim, a constraint, a rationale — has exactly one
authoritative wording at one definition site. Every other appearance is a
**citation**: it may name the home and orient the reader; it may not restate
the content in other words.

A paraphrase is a fork — a second wording that drifts independently, leaving a
reader unable to know which wording is law. Its metastatic form is the
**word cancer** (concept home: MCM `mcm_word_cancer`) — the most recurrent
failure mode of LLM-assisted maintenance of large codebases, and the defect
this clause exists to forbid. A quoin gives a concept one string; this clause
gives a claim one wording — the prose analogue of the prefix-collision
mechanism in `Memos/retired/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`.
Canonical specimen: the 2026-06-10 Diff-Friendly-Prose repair, where a context
file *characterized* a spec's scope instead of citing it, and the
characterization carried the error for months.

**Normative register** rides this clause on the spec side. In a spec, every
sentence either constrains — an implementer or validator could act differently
because it exists — or is explicitly marked as rationale or gestalt (MCM's task
lens `**Gestalt**:` field is the blessed form: metaphor marked as interpretive
guidance). Unmarked ornament is contamination: it spends reader attention and
implies constraint where none exists. A code comment restating design-time
knowledge is the same failure in the other medium (temporal misallocation,
above); flowery spec prose is its mirror.

## The fourth clause — wire formats are named homes

A JSON document this project authors on **both sides** — an envelope, a result
slot, a substitutions blob — is itself a home: a wire format with senders and
receivers that must stay findable from each other. Its keys are therefore
**minted names** (CLAUDE.md mint: any persistent name anywhere is in scope),
carrying one key-sprue per format: `rblv_digest`, never bare `digest`. The sprue
is what makes the format greppable — `grep rblv_` returns the author, every
consumer, and the schema definition, with no central registry to drift (the
`buo` tweak-sprue precedent; the `_RBGL_` substitution registers are the senior
worked example). One sprue per *format*, not per file or per domain: two formats
sharing a sprue blur the census that is the sprue's whole purpose.

The boundary is the Palisade: JSON conforming to a **foreign** schema — a Cloud
Build envelope, an OCI manifest, a vendor API response — keeps the foreign keys.
Sprued keys inside a foreign document are the tell that someone is decorating a
schema they don't own.

The clause is prospective: it binds every wire format as it is authored or
touched; no retroactive sweep is mandated. The conversion move is catalogued as
ACGm_108.

## The move discipline — one litmus, three rules

Every cleanup move obeys one litmus:

> **Mutate only where a *wrong* move is cheaply caught; everywhere else the verb
> is detect-and-report, never repair.**

Code-side moves — the value resolves through a named constant, and shellcheck
plus the fast suite verify it — may mutate now. Spec- and document-side moves are
detect-and-report until the lexer/linter that would catch a wrong move exists.

Three rules ride that litmus:

1. **Triage is part of every move, not an afterthought.** Exclude the
   false-positive class first; declare the authoritative side before any
   alignment; gate scope to settled lanes — never terms a hot heat is still
   moving. A sweep run against vocabulary two heats are still relocating only
   re-creates the drift it meant to kill.
2. **A checker proves itself against a known answer before its output is
   trusted.** The conformance fixture (next section) is the canonical home for
   such checks — ACG eating its own dog food: a named home, referenced not
   recreated.
3. **Concept linkage is name-identity.** The implementing symbol *is* the link: a
   function name equals its quoin's display-text, a tabtarget filename is the
   quoin's sprue, a scoped method is its inlay. A source-to-spec comment is the
   *exception*, reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.

## The named home — the conformance fixture

Rule 2 and move-types ACGm_102/103 all defer to one concrete home: the
**`conformance`** fast-tier theurge fixture
(`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrn_conformance.rs`), registered in the `fast` suite. It
is to evicted vocabulary what BUK's `bug_require_clean_tree` is to the clean-tree
gate — the single named place the check lives, so no caller improvises its own.

The fixture is a data-driven engine, not a word list. It reads rows of
`{kill_stem, keep_contexts}` and scans `Tools/` and `tt/` with identifier-boundary
awareness, flagging a stem as a bare token while sparing it inside a kept
identifier (`Identifier`) or under an exempt path (`PathPrefix`). A row with empty
`keep_contexts` is a *pure corpse* — the term must appear nowhere. The engine
proves itself against known inputs before its verdict on the live tree is trusted
(move discipline, rule 2), so it ships proven with zero production rows.

**The rule: retire a term → add a row there. Never improvise a grep.** Population
is deliberately deferred — each term gains its row behind its own cutover, owned
by the heat retiring it — never against vocabulary a hot heat is still moving
(move discipline, rule 1).

## The Move Catalog

Each move-type states five things: **Detect** (the rule that finds a site),
**Authority** (the ground truth a site is judged against), **Licensing**,
**Verifier**, and a countable **Done**. Headers tag the licensing: **🔧
mutate-now** (a wrong move is cheaply caught — repair in place) or **🔍
detect-only** (read and report; no repair until a verifier exists). Numbered from
101 to leave room for insertion; once a move has a citer it is never renumbered.

### 🔧 ACGm_101: magic-string → constant

- **Detect:** a literal value — a path fragment, a filename, a repeated number or
  string — that *constructs or addresses* something, where a named home exists or
  should. Not human-facing prose.
- **Authority:** the value resolves through a single named constant home (e.g.
  the `RBCC_rbr*_file` family in `rbcc_constants.sh`). The constant is ground
  truth; every literal is a copy that can drift.
- **Licensing:** mutate-now — the constant either resolves or the verifier fails,
  so a wrong move dies loud and immediately.
- **Verifier:** bash — `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh` + `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh`
  green; Rust — `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
- **Done:** every genuine construction site resolves through the constant; the
  constant carries a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
- **Triage:** naming a file *for a human* in a message is not a magic string —
  `doc_params`, `buc_info`/`buh_line` messages, and comments keep the literal.
  Exclude that class before sweeping.
- **Worked example:** the `rbrv.env` filename sweep is the first worked
  application of this move.

### 🔍 ACGm_102: name-identity

- **Detect:** an operation quoin (voiced `//axvo_procedure` or `//axvo_method`)
  whose display-text and implementing symbol disagree.
- **Authority:** the implementing symbol is the link (move discipline, rule 3) —
  a procedure's function name equals its quoin's display-text; a source-to-spec
  comment is the exception where no single symbol can carry it.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side; no mutation until the lexer/linter
  exists.
- **Verifier:** none mechanized in v1 — read and reported by hand. The
  conformance fixture is the future verifier.
- **Done:** a report of operation-quoin / symbol disagreements; nothing mutated.
- **Formal dependency:** full mechanical decidability waits on an AXLA arity
  change — an explicit symbol lookahead on `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method`
  (one `mcm_inlay`, the prefix-disciplined source symbol; mirroring
  `axvo_tabtarget`'s literal lookahead). That change carries a migration across
  every live operation voicing, so it is deferred to its own pace (see *The
  AXLA/MCM interface*). Until it lands, the move is read against the existing
  `axvo_*` voicings — which already mark *which* quoins are operations — plus the
  implementation symbol named at an operation's detail site (`axhems_scoped_method`).

### 🔍 ACGm_103: typed-parameter lookahead

- **Detect:** a parameter whose type is resolvable by bounded lookahead but is
  not yet carried as a type quoin-ref.
- **Authority:** the lookahead scanning rule (below) — the parameter's type is
  the attribute-reference the marker reads.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side.
- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter.
- **Done:** a report of parameters whose type is derivable but unhomed; nothing
  mutated.

### 🔍 ACGm_104: conceptual-comment eviction

- **Detect:** an edit-time comment carrying design-time knowledge — what a thing
  *is*, why it is shaped so, how it relates to other concepts — rather than
  operational mechanics or Palisade characterization.
- **Authority:** the three-homes table. The spec quoin is the design-time home;
  where no quoin exists, the move creates or extends one, then cites it.
- **Licensing:** detect-only for the sweep — relocation mutates two documents
  (source and spec) and a wrong move is not cheaply caught, so each relocation
  lands as its own reviewed move, never batch-applied.
- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter plus spec link validation.
- **Done:** a report of misallocated comments, each tagged with its receiving
  quoin (existing or proposed); relocations land one reviewed move at a time.

### 🔍 ACGm_105: paraphrase collapse

- **Detect:** the same normative idea worded differently in two or more places —
  across specs, context files, handbooks, or comments. The smell: a reader could
  ask "which of these is law?"
- **Authority:** the statements → definition sites clause; the concept home is
  MCM's `mcm_word_cancer` quoin. Triage declares one home authoritative; every
  other site becomes a citation.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — the collapse mutates prose in several documents
  at once and judgment selects the surviving wording; never batch-applied.
- **Verifier:** none mechanized; recurrence is caught by re-running the detect
  sweep against the declared home.
- **Done:** for each word cancer: one authoritative wording at one site,
  citations elsewhere, the variant wordings gone.

### 🔍 ACGm_106: ornament drain

- **Detect:** spec sentences in normative position that neither constrain nor
  carry marked rationale or gestalt — prose an implementer or validator could
  delete without acting differently.
- **Authority:** the normative-register rule (the statements clause, above).
- **Licensing:** detect-only — whether a sentence constrains is judgment;
  draining is per-document and reviewed.
- **Verifier:** none in v1.
- **Done:** the spec reads as constraint plus marked rationale; deleted ornament
  is gone, demoted ornament is marked.

### 🔍 ACGm_107: skidmark drain

- **Detect:** version commentary in a durable document — revision markers, dated
  change narration, "new in vX" annotations. Durable means spec, guide, code, or
  context file; a dated artifact (memo, scar, commit message) is exempt — history
  is its content.
- **Authority:** the history-time row of the three-homes table; the concept home
  is MCM's `mcm_skidmark` quoin. The commit is the home — git log and blame carry
  the change story with a forcing function the document body lacks.
- **Licensing:** detect-only as a sweep; an individual drain is mutate-now when
  the deleted text is pure narration (the content survives in git), reviewed when
  narration is entangled with normative statements.
- **Verifier:** none in v1; candidate for a cheap regex pass in the future linter
  (date-shaped tokens and version markers outside memo paths).
- **Done:** durable documents read in the present tense; history questions answer
  through git.

### 🔧 ACGm_108: bare wire-key → minted sprue

- **Detect:** an RB-authored JSON wire format (both sender and receiver ours)
  whose keys are bare terms (`name`, `digest`, `stamp`) rather than sprue-carried
  minted names.
- **Authority:** the fourth clause (wire formats are named homes). The sprue
  itself is governed by the mint discipline (CLAUDE.md): one sprue per format,
  terminal-exclusive, used for nothing else.
- **Licensing:** mutate-now when the format's author and every consumer convert
  in one move and a live verifier covers the path — a rename either resolves
  everywhere or the suite fails loud. Detect-only when the consumers cannot all
  convert together; for an RB-authored format that inability is itself a finding
  worth recording.
- **Verifier:** the consuming suite tier for the format (e.g. the capture
  fixtures for the Lode vouch envelope), plus the census check: `grep <sprue>_`
  returns exactly the format's sites and nothing else.
- **Done:** every key in the format carries the sprue; the format's schema
  identifier (if it has one) is bumped; the sprue grep is the format's complete
  sender/receiver census. First worked application: the Lode vouch envelope
  (`rblv_`, schema `rbld-vouch-2`). Second worked application: the Lode slot/result
  format (`rbls_`, keys `rbls_slot_1`/`rbls_stamp`/`rbls_vouch`).
- **Triage:** foreign-schema documents are excluded by the Palisade boundary
  before any sweep. Pre-MVP formats convert with no dual-dialect reading — there
  is no compatibility surface; standing artifacts are re-captured or discarded,
  never grandfathered.

## The AXLA/MCM interface

### The lookahead scanning rule

When counting a marker's lookahead in an MCM document, scan the following text and
**skip prose lines and blank lines; count only attribute-references (`{…}` linked
terms) and backtick sprues.** A dimension that counts "the Nth linked term" (e.g.
`axd_grouped`) counts attribute-references only — a backtick inlay or sprue is a
distinct token type and does not shift that count.

### Name-identity's symbol-link dependency (deferred)

ACGm_102 becomes mechanically decidable only when an operation voicing carries its
implementing symbol explicitly. The surveyed shape: add a one-`mcm_inlay`
lookahead to `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method` reading the literal source symbol
(the MCM-correct token for a prefix-disciplined name, matching how
`axhems_scoped_method` already reads an implementation method name). It is an
*amendment* to two established voicings with many live consumers, not a fresh
mint, so it carries a real migration and is held for its own deliberate pace
rather than folded into guide authoring. Recorded here as ACGm_102's named
dependency; raised and deferred, not dropped.

## Related Guides

- **BCG** — host bash. Constant Discipline and Interface Contamination are the
  bash instances of the spine.
- **RCG** — host Rust. Constant Discipline (Beyond Strings), String Boundary
  Discipline, and Interface Contamination are the Rust instances.
- **WSG**, **CBG** — foreign-environment siblings (Windows transport, cloud step).
- **MCM** — builds the named homes ACG references; the design-time home.
- **AXLA** — the motif vocabulary; the lookahead scanning rule and the
  name-identity symbol-link dependency live against it.
- **CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline" (mint)** — governs the naming homes
  themselves.

## Acronym Registry

| Term | Expansion |
|------|-----------|
| ACG | Allocation Coding Guide (this document) |
| The spine | "Reference the home" — values → constants, concepts → quoin-refs, statements → definition sites |
| Wire format | An RB-authored JSON document with senders and receivers; a named home whose keys are minted (sprued) names — the fourth clause |
| Word cancer | Variations of one normative idea scattered as paraphrases, each drifting independently; the metastatic violation of statements → definition sites. Quoin home: MCM `mcm_word_cancer` |
| Normative register | Spec-side rule: every sentence constrains or is marked rationale/gestalt; unmarked ornament is contamination |
| Skidmark | Version commentary stranded in a durable document; history-time knowledge whose home is the commit. Quoin home: MCM `mcm_skidmark` |
| Home | The one right location for a fact, fixed by when it is read (design / edit / execution time) |
| Move-type | A catalogued `ACGm_` cleanup move: detect-rule, authority, licensing, verifier, countable Done |
| mutate-now | Licensing: a wrong move is cheaply caught, so the verb may repair in place |
| detect-only | Licensing: the verb reads and reports; no repair until a verifier exists |
| Name-identity | Concept linkage where the implementing symbol *is* the link to its quoin |
